headline: Medical Tourism Revenue Hits RM2.4 Billion While Public System Struggles
context: Malaysia's medical tourism industry generated RM2.4 billion in 2025 as resources are directed toward international patients.

cards.big: Medical tourism earned RM2.4 billion in 2025 while public hospitals face specialist shortages
cards.sub: 1.3 million international patients treated at private Malaysian hospitals last year

cards.big: 68% of medical tourists use private hospitals that employ 42% of Malaysia's specialists
cards.sub: Specialist concentration in private tourism-focused hospitals drains public system capacity

cards.big: MHTC targets RM5 billion in medical tourism revenue by 2030 requiring more specialist capacity
cards.sub: Growth targets compete directly with public healthcare human resource needs

cards.big: India mandates that private hospitals allocate 25% of beds to subsidized local patients
cards.sub: Cross-subsidization model ensures local communities benefit from medical tourism growth

cards.big: Exporting healthcare while citizens queue for months exposes a troubling priority disorder
Like a restaurant that serves tourists a five-course meal while the kitchen staff eat leftovers — same building, different service tiers
cards.sub: Specialist capacity diverted to medical tourists directly extends the wait times for public patients

cards.big: Should medical tourism hospitals be required to allocate specialist hours to public patients
